evals.expected_output: Full harness audit report with metrics table (global skills count, agents count, hook entries, proposal backlog, days since audit), findings by severity (CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW), gap analysis section, and ranked proposals table. Should detect current harness state accurately — skill count should match actual directory count, org should be detected as Klever from cwd.

assertions.name: produces_metrics_table
assertions.description: Report contains a metrics summary table

assertions.name: accurate_skill_count
assertions.script: count=$(ls -d ~/.claude/skills/*/SKILL.md 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' '); echo "Expected ~$count global skills"; grep -oP 'Global skills\s*\|\s*\K[0-9]+' output.md | head -1
assertions.description: Reported global skill count matches actual directory count

assertions.name: detects_org_as_klever
assertions.description: Detects current org as Klever from working directory

assertions.name: has_findings_section
assertions.description: Report includes findings organized by severity

assertions.name: checks_proposal_backlog
assertions.description: Reports on skill proposal backlog status

evals.prompt: I feel like my setup is getting bloated, can you do a quick check on my skills and hooks? don't need the full analysis, just the numbers and any red flags
evals.expected_output: Quick-mode report: metrics table and drift detection only. No gap analysis section. Should be noticeably shorter than the full report. Should still catch broken hooks and org bleeding.

assertions.name: produces_metrics
assertions.description: Still produces metrics even in quick mode

assertions.name: no_gap_analysis
assertions.description: Quick mode skips the gap analysis section

evals.prompt: run a harness audit and fix anything that's obviously broken — like dead hooks or skills pointing to missing files
evals.expected_output: Full report followed by interactive apply mode. For each proposed fix, should present what will change, ask for confirmation (apply/skip/stop). Should identify any broken hooks (scripts that don't exist on disk) as CRITICAL and propose removing them first.

assertions.name: enters_apply_mode
assertions.description: Enters interactive apply mode after the report

assertions.name: asks_before_acting
assertions.description: Does not auto-apply fixes without user confirmation
